name: faas-add-api
description: Conversational API authoring for projects/faas-platform. Takes a natural-language description plus mission metadata, produces a requirements draft, a design spec, a task plan, executes the plan via Claude Code sub-agent (full claude_code tool preset, cwd pinned to the project), and reports gate-check results. Deploy + approval are run as separate downstream pipelines (release-package → deploy-release) so the human approval moment stays explicit.

params.additional_context: Target project: FaaS platform (projects/faas-platform). Every API is a Lambda (Node.js 22) behind API Gateway HTTP API, with Aurora Global MySQL for state and the faas-runtime layer for shared concerns. Produce a design that names exactly one Lambda handler, at most one migration file (additive-only), and the CDK binding update. Respect the api-spec.schema.json contract. Components should include COMP-HANDLER, COMP-MIGRATION (if needed), COMP-REGISTRY_WRITE (always), COMP-CDK_BINDING. Open decisions must flag blocking=true when the request is ambiguous about auth_policy, data mode (read/write), or rate limits.

params.message: faas-add-api done: requirements v{{requirements.version}} / design v{{design.version}} (architecture_ready={{design.architecture_ready.passed}}) / task plan v{{task_plan.version}} ({{task_plan.task_count}} tasks, ready={{task_plan.task_plan_ready.passed}}) / execution succeeded={{execution.succeeded}}/{{execution.total_tasks}}. Next: pipelines/code-review-cycle.json → pipelines/release-package.json → pipelines/deploy-release.json.
